text:
Your budget app knows your salary, your rent, where you eat, what you buy your kids, and your guilty pleasures.

Now ask yourself: who else has access to that data?

I built BudgetVault because the answer should be "nobody."

#privacy #budgeting #personalfinance

I used to pay $14/month for a budgeting app. It was good. But budgeting is a skill — once you learn it, why keep paying?

So I built BudgetVault. $14.99. Once. Learn the envelope method, build the habit, move on.

#budgetapp #personalfinance #moneytips

Envelope budgeting in 30 seconds:

1. Get paid
2. Split money into categories (rent, food, fun)
3. Only spend what's in each envelope
4. When an envelope is empty, you're done

That's it. No spreadsheets. No formulas. Just clarity.

#envelopebudgeting #moneytips #budgeting101

How do you budget?

A) Envelope method
B) 50/30/20 rule
C) Check my balance and hope
D) Spreadsheet I abandon after 2 weeks

No wrong answers.

#personalfinance #budgeting

I spent 2 years building a budgeting app.

The hardest decision? Processing everything on-device instead of using a cloud backend.

10x harder to build. But your financial data is worth that tradeoff.

#indiedev #iosdev #buildinpublic

actual_text:
BudgetVault launches tomorrow.

2 years of work. Here's the pitch:

— Envelope budgeting on your device
— On-device ML insights
— $14.99 once. No subscription.
— No account. No data collection.

Link tomorrow at 9 AM EST.

#applaunch #ios #budgetvault

BudgetVault is live on the App Store.

Free to download. $14.99 once to unlock everything.

No account. No subscription. No data ever leaves your iPhone.

2 years of work. Here it is.

#budgetvault #applaunch #budgetapp
Download here — free to try, no account needed.

What makes it different:

I didn't want to run servers. I didn't want your data. I didn't want to charge you every month.

Everything runs on your phone. $14.99 once. Apple verified: "Data Not Collected."

That's it.

text:
Things budget apps ask for that BudgetVault doesn't:

- Email address
- Bank login
- Phone number
- Location data
- Contact list access

I don't even have a server to store that stuff on.

#dataprivacy #budgetapp #personalfinance

BudgetVault has on-device ML that learns your spending patterns.

"You spend 40% more on food the week after payday."

No cloud. No API calls. Your phone figures it out locally.

#coreml #ondeviceAI #budgeting

"Hey Siri, log $14 for lunch."

Expense logged in BudgetVault without opening the app.

Siri Shortcuts, widgets, Face ID lock. Built to feel like iOS, not a web app in disguise.

#iosapp #siri #budgetapp

Budget tip: Your daily allowance matters more than your monthly budget.

$3,000/month feels abstract.
$68/day after bills feels real.

BudgetVault calculates this automatically and updates every time you spend.

#budgetingtips #moneytips #personalfinance

Hot take: most budgeting apps are designed to make you feel guilty.

Red numbers. Warning alerts. "You exceeded your dining budget!"

BudgetVault shows what's left in each envelope and lets you decide.

Your money. Your choices.

#budgetapp #personalfinance

BudgetVault is $14.99 right now. That's the current price.

It's going up once I ship the next round of features.

If you've been thinking about it, now is the time.

#budgetapp #personalfinance #iosapp

I opened a popular budgeting app yesterday. Before logging one expense:

- Create account
- Verify email
- Choose a plan
- Enter payment info
- Connect your bank

BudgetVault: open the app. Start budgeting.

#budgetvault #iosapp #privacyfirst

One week since launch.

People are genuinely surprised when an app doesn't ask for their email.

That says everything about how broken app defaults are.

Week 2: listening to feedback and shipping updates fast.

#indiedev #buildinpublic #budgetvault
